texto: A few weeks after my mom passed in November of 2013, I came back from an injury and entered the Egg Bowl in the second half against Ole Miss. I'll never forget the feeling when I walked back out on the field. As I walked into the Egg Bowl, the crowd stood up and clapped like they were enveloping me in a giant hug.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Dak Prescott: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “A few weeks after my mom passed in November of 2013, I came back from an injury and entered the E…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, november, me pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de me e never-forget.

texto: Back when I was a senior in high school in Haughton, LA, I had a chance to go to LSU. Everyone I grew up with adored LSU, including my mom. But I chose to come to Mississippi State because I wanted to start a new tradition instead of perpetuating an established one.

frase_original: Back when I was a senior in high school in Haughton, LA, I had a chance to go to LSU. Everyone I grew up with adored LSU, including my mom. But I chose to come to Mississippi State because I wanted to start a new tradition instead of perpetuating an established one.
